target.title: fix(gateway): restore all-agent session discovery in sessions.usage when no agentId is provided
target.body:
## Summary

- Backend: when no `agentId` is provided in `sessions.usage` requests, discover sessions across all configured agents instead of defaulting to "main"
- Frontend: add `usageAgentId` state and agent selector dropdown to the usage page so operators can optionally scope usage queries to a specific agent
- Fixes the Web UI usage page agent filter that was broken since v2026.5.17 (commit 6a12c6f79915 / PR #81386)

## Behavior addressed

The v2026.5.17 fix scoped `sessions.usage` lookups by `agentId`, defaulting to "main" when no `agentId` was specified. The Web UI frontend never sent `agentId` in API requests, so non-main agent sessions were never returned, and the client-side agent filter popover only saw main sessions.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** sessions.usage API defaulted to "main" agent when no agentId was provided, breaking the Web UI usage page for multi-agent setups. Non-main agent sessions were invisible.

**Real environment tested:** pnpm test on macOS 26, Node 24, openclaw dev checkout at commit ff724402b6 on branch fix/usage-agent-filter-87132.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/usage.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** All 62 tests pass across 6 test suites. The key behavioral test `returns sessions across all agents when no agentId is specified` verifies that `discoverAllSessions` is called for each configured agent (main + opus) instead of being scoped to main only. The `loadCombinedSessionStoreForGateway` is called without agentId filtering, confirming store loading is no longer scoped to a default agent.

**Observed result after fix:** Backend tests confirm that when no agentId is provided, sessions are discovered across all agents (2 discoverAllSessions calls for main and opus) rather than scoped to main only. When agentId IS provided (e.g., `agentId: "main"`), scoping still works correctly. Frontend controller tests confirm `usageAgentId` is properly passed through to the sessions.usage API request.

**What was not tested:** Full end-to-end browser testing with a live multi-agent gateway was not performed. The agent selector dropdown UX was verified through code review but not through browser-based Playwright/integration tests.

Fixes #87132

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: nianjiuzst

commands.author: eleqtrizit

target.title: fix(gateway): enable default auth rate limiting
target.body:
Summary
- Enables the existing gateway auth rate limiter by default when `gateway.auth.rateLimit` is not configured.
- Keeps normal loopback exemption behavior for local clients and preserves the separate browser-origin limiter behavior.

Changes
- Create the general gateway auth limiter with built-in defaults instead of leaving it undefined without explicit config.
- Dispose the now-unconditional general auth limiter during gateway shutdown.
- Add a WebSocket regression test for no-Origin remote auth failures using a trusted forwarded client IP.

Validation
Behavior addressed: Default remote non-browser gateway WebSocket auth failures are throttled instead of bypassing rate-limit checks when no explicit `gateway.auth.rateLimit` config exists.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout, isolated temp config/state, real gateway server started from source with `startGatewayServer`, and real `ws` clients. This proof did not use the Vitest gateway harness. The proof config intentionally omitted `gateway.auth.rateLimit`, configured `gateway.trustedProxies: ["127.0.0.1"]`, and used `X-Forwarded-For: 203.0.113.50` to exercise the non-loopback no-Origin client path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with an inline script that wrote an isolated config, started a gateway on a random loopback port, made 11 wrong-token no-Origin WebSocket auth attempts with `x-forwarded-for: 203.0.113.50`, then made 12 direct loopback no-Origin wrong-token attempts. Also reran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.auth.browser-hardening.test.ts` and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
[setup]
config_path=/tmp/openclaw-rate-limit-proof-UYuns1/openclaw.json
state_dir=/tmp/openclaw-rate-limit-proof-UYuns1/state
gateway_url=ws://127.0.0.1:34055
gateway.auth.rateLimit_present=false
gateway.trustedProxies=127.0.0.1
programmatic_origin_header=absent
remote_client_ip_via_x_forwarded_for=203.0.113.50

[remote no-Origin client via trusted X-Forwarded-For]
attempt=01 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=02 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=03 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=04 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=05 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=06 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=07 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=08 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=09 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=10 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=11 outcome=RATE_LIMITED detail=n/a message=unauthorized: too many failed authentication attempts (retry later)
summary auth_errors=10 rate_limited=1

[direct loopback no-Origin client]
attempt=01 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=02 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=03 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=04 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=05 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=06 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=07 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=08 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=09 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=10 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=11 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
attempt=12 outcome=AUTH_ERROR detail=n/a message=unauthorized: gateway token mismatch (provide gateway auth token)
summary auth_errors=12 rate_limited=0

[result]
remote_expected_lockout_after_10=PASS
loopback_exemption_preserved=PASS
```

Focused regression output:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  14 passed (14)
```

Observed result after fix: With `gateway.auth.rateLimit` absent, a remote no-Origin WebSocket client is locked out on the 11th wrong-token attempt. A direct loopback no-Origin client remains exempt from the default limiter across 12 wrong-token attempts.

What was not tested: Full repository test/check suites, packaged install/update flows, and a remote host outside this local checkout. Docs/audit alignment for the default-on policy is not changed in this PR.

Notes
- AI-assisted with Codex.
- Release-note context: gateway auth hardening now applies built-in throttling to remote programmatic auth attempts by default while preserving local loopback behavior.
